explanation: This command creates a scheduled task using the suspicious schedule type 'ONCE' and runs it with high privileges ('NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM'), matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command uses the short form of the user parameter ('SYSTEM') instead of the full name ('NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM'), which is still recognized by schtasks and matches the Sigma rule.
This command uses quotes around the user parameter ('SYSTEM'), which is a safe mutation technique and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command adds an optional '/f' (force) flag, which does not affect the detection criteria but demonstrates reordering and omission of optional elements.
This command uses 'HIGHEST' as the user privilege level, which is another valid value for the '/ru' parameter and matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
